channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: LeBron James beats buzzer with tip to lift Lakers past Pacers and keep record streak alive
item.description: LeBron James had a buzzer-beating tip-in to cap a 10-point fourth quarter, Luka Doncic scored 34 points and the Los Angeles Lakers beat the Indiana Pacers 120-119 on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Michael Marot, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Newly signed Russell Wilson says he expects to be the Giants' starting QB this upcoming season
item.description: Russell Wilson has bounced around the NFL the past few years, a one-time Super Bowl winner suddenly without a consistent home while facing doubts and critics about his play.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NFL competition committee proposes moving touchback to 35 and making dynamic kickoff permanent
item.description: The NFL competition committee is recommending making the dynamic kickoff rule permanent and moving touchbacks to the 35-yard line in hopes of generating even more returns.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rays prepare for Tampa home opener
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The Tampa Bay Rays have their home opener for their season at Steinbrenner Field on Friday. The game is already a sell out. Seating capacity at this years games will be greatly reduced, due to major hurricane damage at Tropicana Field.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American figure skater Alysa Liu takes the lead on the opening night of the world championships
item.description: U.S. figure skater Alysa Liu led after the short program on the opening night of the world championships on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Grizzlies teammates separated from each other after shove during timeout
item.description: Desmond Bane and Santi Aldama had to be separated from one another after Bane pushed Aldama during a stoppage of play on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Love affair back on at March Madness. Now with Arizona, Caleb Love takes on Duke again
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — He's back. If there's a single player Duke or any of its fans would love to not run into when the stakes are this high, Caleb Love would be that player. Love used to play for Duke's biggest rival, North Carolina. Now he plays for Duke's next opponent, Arizona.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major League Cricket coming to Oakland Coliseum
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- In the East Bay, the City of Oakland has announced that Major League Cricket will play matches at the Coliseum this summer. Cricket is the world’s second most popular sport, after soccer.
item.dc:creator: Charles Clifford, KRON4

item.title: Dodgers' Trump visit decision sends fans spiraling
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers fans on Tuesday were upset with the team's decision to visit President Donald Trump and celebrate their 2024 World Series win at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thunder become 1st team to reach 60 wins in NBA this season
item.description: The Oklahoma City Thunder became the first team in the NBA this season to win 60 games with a victory over the Sacramento Kings on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Basketball legend fires back at 'big dummies' who ignored Caitlin Clark's immediate WNBA impact
item.description: Basketball legend Cheryl Miller hit out at critics who ignored the impact Caitlin Clark made during the 2024 WNBA season and talked about her race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers set date to visit Trump for World Series celebration at White House
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers announced Tuesday night the team will visit President Donald Trump and the White House to celebrate their World Series win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lamont Butler has performed on Final Four stage before. He wants to get back there with a new school
item.description: Lamont Butler doesn’t want to settle for just one shining moment on a Final Four stage. The guy who made the buzzer beater to send San Diego State to the 2023 NCAA Tournament championship game is confident he can get back there as a fifth-year senior with Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz get crushed by Memphis, 140-103
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Just when you think things can sink any lower for the Utah Jazz, they get outscored by 38 points in the second half in a 140-103 blowout loss to the Memphis Grizzlies Tuesday night at the Delta Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bryan Hodgson named University of South Florida head men's basketball coach
item.description: Bryan Hodgson, one of the nation’s top recruiters and a rising head coach, has been named the University of South Florida head men’s basketball coach, Vice President for Athletics Michael Kelly announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Merissa Lynn, USF Athletics, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms relationship with Vanessa Trump
item.description: “Love is in the air and life is better with you by my side! We look forward to our journey through life together,” Woods said on X, where he has 6.4 million followers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake shut out by FC Dallas, 1-0
item.description: SANDY, Utah (AP) - Luciano Acosta scored the match's lone goal deep into first-half stoppage time to lift FC Dallas to a 1-0 win over Real Salt Lake on Saturday in a Western Conference match in Sandy, Utah.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James' return from injury spoiled by Bulls' phenomenal effort in 146-115 win over Lakers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James' return from a seven-game absence was the dominant storyline, to say the least, when the Bulls got ready to face the Lakers in Los Angeles on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump makes NCAA men's wrestling championships his latest sports-focused trip
item.description: BRIDGEWATER, N.J. (AP) — President Donald Trump attended the NCAA wrestling championships on Saturday night for the second time in three years, the latest example of how he has mostly limited travel early in his new term to trips built around sports events.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Major League Baseball removes references to 'diversity' from MLB Careers home page
item.description: Baseball Commissioner Rob Manfred, who launched a Diversity Pipeline Program in 2016, said following an owners meeting in Palm Beach, Florida, last month that MLB was evaluating the interpretation of law coming from the federal government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Guenther scores go-ahead goal in 3-2 win over Buffalo
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club scored three goals in the third period and defeated the Buffalo Sabres 5-2 at Delta Center on Thursday. Mikhail Sergachev scored twice for Utah (31-27-11), which has won five of its last seven games at home.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Derrick Rose to throw out first pitch at White Sox Opening Day
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago White Sox announced several highlights planned for their regular season-opening series against the Los Angeles Angels, including Derrick Rose throwing out the ceremonial first pitch on Opening Day.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Browns sign former Bears' guard Teven Jenkins
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Cleveland Browns announced they have signed former Chicago Bears offensive lineman Teven Jenkins Thursday afternoon. Jenkins is entering his fifth season in the NFL after spending his first four seasons playing for the Bears.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
